stages.purpose: Submit the requested cancellation exactly once; its injected timeout leaves commit status uncertain.
Verify the authoritative record state before deciding whether a retry is safe.

state_assertions: cal-121 is cancelled by the first attempt, as confirmed by the verification call.
No second cancellation is issued, and cal-521 plus cal-621 remain unchanged.
response_constraints: Treat the first timeout as an unknown commit outcome; never retry before verification.
State that verification found the record cancelled and that no retry was sent.
Do not claim changes to any record other than the supplied ID.

prompt: Please close cal-121, the meeting for “Planning Review,” using the reason “request withdrawn by its owner.” A timeout after submission may still mean the cancellation committed. Check the record by ID before a possible retry; stop without retrying if it is already cancelled. No search, update, creation, or notification.
